title: Physopsis viscida

uses: Physopsis viscida, also known as the sticky mouse-ear or sticky cat's-ear, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and has been used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions and wounds. In some regions, it is believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can aid in the healing process of cuts and abrasions. Additionally, the plant has been used in some traditional remedies to treat conditions like fever and rheumatism. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Physopsis viscida for medicinal purposes.
